title: Freezing Meats and Dairy
content:
MEAT, POULTRY, FISH

Freeze quickly at 0°F or below. Wrap moisture-vapor-proof material; exclude air. Freeze in meal-size portions. Label with cut and date.

Meats: Wrap tightly; freeze quickly. Ground meat: use within 3-4 months. Steaks, roasts: 6-12 months.

Poultry: Clean, dressed, wrapped. Freeze whole or cut up. Use within 12 months.

Fish: Clean, scale, remove entrails. Wrap tightly; freeze quickly. Use within 3-6 months.

DAIRY PRODUCTS

Butter: Freeze in original carton, overwrapped. Good 6-9 months.

Cheese: Hard cheeses freeze better than soft. Freeze in small portions. Texture may become crumbly; use for cooking.

Milk: Pour off ½ cup per pint to allow expansion. Texture changes—use for cooking only after freezing.

Eggs: Do not freeze in shell. Beat whites and yolks separately; package with labeling; freeze. Or beat together with ½ teaspoon salt or 1½ teaspoons sugar per cup (label which). Use within 12 months.

Cream: Whipped cream freezes well. Sweetened and flavored cream freezes satisfactorily. Unwhipped cream separates—use for cooking only.

BAKED GOODS

Most breads, cakes, cookies, pies freeze well. Cool completely before freezing. Wrap moisture-vapor-proof material; exclude air.

Breads: Freeze in loaf or slices. Thaw in wrapping at room temperature. Refresh in 350°F oven 10-15 minutes.

Cakes: Freeze unfrosted or frosted (frosting acts as protective coating). Thaw in wrapping.

Cookies: Freeze baked cookies in rigid containers. Or freeze cookie dough shaped into rolls; slice and bake without thawing.

Pies: Unbaked fruit pies freeze well. Bake from frozen state (add 15-20 minutes). Baked cream or custard pies do not freeze well.

STORAGE TIMES AT 0°F

Fruits: 12 months
Vegetables: 8-12 months
Meat (roasts, steaks): 6-12 months
Ground meat: 3-4 months
Poultry: 12 months
Fish: 3-6 months
Butter: 6-9 months
Baked goods: 2-3 months

THAWING

Most foods: Thaw in refrigerator (slow, safe) or at room temperature in wrapping. Vegetables: Cook without thawing (except corn on cob—thaw partially). Fruits: Thaw in refrigerator or at room temperature. Meat: Thaw in refrigerator; cook promptly after thawing.
